name: Castle of Good Hope
description: A 17th-century bastion fort, the oldest surviving colonial building in South Africa.

name: Groote Kerk
description: South Africa's oldest Protestant church, a grand Dutch Reformed church with a magnificent pipe organ.

name: St. George's Cathedral
description: Anglican cathedral where Desmond Tutu served as Archbishop; a symbol of anti-apartheid resistance.

name: South African National Gallery
description: Housed in a beautiful Edwardian building, showcasing South African and international art.

name: Company's Garden
description: The oldest garden in South Africa, laid out by the Dutch East India Company as a vegetable garden.

name: Iziko South African Museum
description: Established in 1825, the oldest museum in South Africa with extensive natural and cultural history collections.

name: The Old Synagogue
description: Former Orthodox synagogue, now part of the Iziko Museums; the oldest synagogue building in South Africa.

name: Wonderwerk Cave
description: Archaeological site with evidence of human occupation spanning over a million years; a National Heritage Site.

name: St. Mary's Cathedral
description: The oldest Roman Catholic cathedral in South Africa, featuring stunning Gothic Revival architecture.

name: Fort Wynyard
description: Coastal fortification built to protect Table Bay, now part of the Simons Town Naval Base.

name: Old Town House
description: One of the oldest buildings in Cape Town, served as the city hall and magistrate's court in the 18th century.

name: Dutch Reformed Church, Simonstown
description: A charming example of early 19th-century Cape Dutch architecture with a distinctive bell tower.

name: V&A Waterfront Clock Tower
description: Historic clock tower and tidal swimming pool built by the Victorian waterfront developers.

name: Westernboord Dutch Reformed Church
description: Stunning Cape Dutch church with a thatched roof, representing traditional Afrikaner architecture.

name: Moederkerk, Stellenbosch
description: Historical Dutch Reformed church in the heart of Stellenbosch, featuring classic Cape Dutch design.

name: Stellenbosch University Old Main Building
description: Historic icon of higher education in South Africa, built in the Victorian Dutch Renaissance style.

name: Vergelegen Estate
description: Historic Cape Dutch estate with beautifully preserved gardens and architecture dating back to 1700.

name: Groot Constantia
description: South Africa's oldest wine estate, with a magnificent manor house dating to the 17th century.

name: Durban City Hall
description: Edwardian Baroque municipal building with an impressive clock tower, dominating the city skyline.

name: Emmanuel Cathedral
description: Roman Catholic cathedral with twin spires, serving as the seat of the Archbishop of Durban.

name: Fort Amherst
description: Coastal fort built by the British to protect Durban harbour; now a popular viewpoint and historic site.

name: Old Durban Railway Station
description: Victorian-era railway station with a prominent clock tower, now part of the Durban transport hub.

name: Shri Veerabhadra Siva Temple
description: Oldest Hindu temple in South Africa, a vibrant cultural monument reflecting Indian heritage.

name: Market Square, Durban
description: Historic square surrounded by Victorian and Edwardian buildings, including the City Hall and Supreme Court.

name: Jeppe High School for Boys
description: Heritage school building in classic Edwardian style, one of the oldest schools in Johannesburg.

name: St. Mary's Anglican Cathedral
description: Gothic Revival cathedral and the seat of the Anglican Diocese of Johannesburg.

name: Johannesburg City Hall
description: Renaissance Revival civic building with a 972-pipe organ; a National Heritage Site.

name: Supreme Court of Appeal
description: Imposing Victorian building housing South Africa's highest court on non-constitutional matters.

name: National Women's Monument
description: Pioneering monument sculpted by Anton van Wouw, commemorating the suffering of women and children during the Anglo-Boer War.

name: Old Presidency
description: Former residence of the president of the Orange Free State; now a museum with period furnishings.

name: Freshford House
description: Victorian pleasure lodge with intricate woodwork and ornate ironwork; exceptionally well-preserved.

name: St. Andrew's Presbyterian Church
description: Handsome sandstone Gothic church where a young J.R.R. Tolkien was married in 1916.

name: Oliewenhuis Art Museum
description: Housed in a magnificent Cape Dutch mansion, the second largest art museum in South Africa.

name: Union Buildings
description: Seat of the South African government, designed by Sir Herbert Baker; iconic twin-domed Edwardian architecture.

name: Pretoria City Hall
description: Imposing civic building with a 80-meter clock tower, representing Cape Dutch and neo-Renaissance styles.

name: Melrose House
description: Victorian mansion where the Treaty of Vereeniging was signed in 1902, ending the Anglo-Boer War.

name: Church Square
description: Historic public square surrounded by historic buildings and featuring statues of Paul Kruger and others.

name: St. Alban's Anglican Cathedral
description: Gothic Revival cathedral built from Transvaal bluestone; seat of the Anglican Diocese of Pretoria.

name: Fort Schanskop
description: One of four forts built to defend Pretoria during the Anglo-Boer War; now a military museum.

name: Paul Kruger House
description: Former residence of President Paul Kruger of the South African Republic; now a museum preserving his legacy.

name: Rust en Vreugd
description: Historic Cape Dutch house with beautiful gardens, now a cultural center and art venue.

name: Khaya la Bantu
description: Historic site commemorating the 1820 Settlers and indigenous-Xhosa interactions; significant cultural landscape.

name: Groote Post
description: Historic inn and post office along the Swartland route; Cape Dutch architecture with later additions.

name: Drostdy, Swellendam
description: Historic Magistrate's residence built in 1747, now a museum showcasing Overberg heritage.

name: Aasvoëlkop
description: Heritage site with iron-age ruins and panoramic views over Pretoria; significant archaeological location.

name: Thaba Bosiu
description: Historic sandstone summit that served as the stronghold of Moshoeshoe I, founder of the Basotho nation; a National Heritage Site.

name: President Steyn Statue
description: Bronze statue of President Martinus Theunis Steyn, relocated to the Anglo-Boer War Museum in 2020. Originally at the University of the Free State.

name: Nelson Mandela Statue (Cape Town)
description: 9m bronze Mandela statue on the balcony of the Cape Town City Hall, where he gave his first public speech after release from prison.

name: Devil's Peak Earthworks
description: Remnants of British defensive fortifications on Devil's Peak during the Second Boer War; now part of the Table Mountain National Park.

name: Great Synagogue (Cape Town)
description: Historic synagogue on admission Street, an important cultural landmark of the Cape Town Jewish community.

name: St. James Catholic Church
description: Small historic church in St. James, a reminder of the early 20th-century seaside community development.

name: Gugulethu Seven Memorial
description: Memorial honoring seven anti-apartheid activists killed by police in 1986; a symbol of resistance and remembrance in Gugulethu.

name: Bo-Kaap Museum
description: Housed in the oldest surviving Bo-Kaap house, showcasing the cultural heritage and traditions of the Cape Malay community.

name: Zeitz Museum of Contemporary Art Africa
description: Groundbreaking museum of contemporary African art, housed in a repurposed grain silo at the V&A Waterfront.

name: District Six Museum
description: Museum in a former Methodist church in District Six, Cape Town. Memorial to 60,000 residents forced from their homes during apartheid removals in the 1970s.

name: Robben Island
description: Island off the coast of Cape Town, used as a prison during apartheid. Nelson Mandela was incarcerated here for 18 years. Now a UNESCO World Heritage Site and museum.

name: Apartheid Museum
description: Museum in Johannesburg illustrating apartheid and 20th-century South African history. Opened 2001, part of Gold Reef City complex.

name: Voortrekker Monument
description: Massive granite monument in Pretoria commemorating the Voortrekkers who left the Cape Colony in the 1830s–40s. Unveiled 1949, designed by Gerard Moerdijk.

name: Anglo-Boer War Memorial
description: Originally called the Rand Regiments Memorial, dedicated to the men of the Witwatersrand who joined as British soldiers in the Rand Regiments and lost their lives during the Second Boer War (1899–1902). Rededicated on 10 October 1999 to all people who died during the war, located in Johannesburg.

name: Sarel Cilliers monument
description: Monument to Sarel Cilliers (1801-1871), Voortrekker leader and preacher. Located at Doornkloof farm near Lindley, Free State. Cilliers was a significant figure in the Great Trek and the Battle of Blood River.

name: National Women's Monument
description: The National Women's Monument (Afrikaans: Nasionale Vrouemonument) in Bloemfontein commemorates the roughly 27,000 Boer women and children who died in British concentration camps during the Second Boer War. A Provincial Heritage Site in the Free State.

name: Anglo-Boer War Museum
description: The Anglo-Boer War Museum (War Museum) is the only museum in the world dedicated solely to the Anglo-Boer War (1899-1902). It features unique art collections, dioramas, and exhibits that bring visitors closer to understanding the war's background. The National Women's Monument is situated at the same location.

name: National Museum, Bloemfontein
description: The National Museum in Bloemfontein dates back to the 1870s, established on 20 July 1877 and opened to the public on 20 May 1878. It has developed into an institution of international stature, focusing on natural history, cultural history and art, with 13 research departments and several satellite museums including Oliewenhuis Art Museum and Freshford House Museum.

name: Sacred Heart Cathedral, Bloemfontein
description: The Sacred Heart Cathedral is a Roman Catholic cathedral located in Bloemfontein, Free State. It serves as the main church and seat of the Catholic Archdiocese of Bloemfontein, created in 1951.

name: Cradle of Humankind
description: Cradle of Humankind, a monument in Gauteng.

name: Robben Island
description: Robben Island, a monument in Western Cape.

name: Mapungubwe Cultural Landscape
description: Mapungubwe Cultural Landscape, a monument in Limpopo.

name: Taung Fossil Site
description: Taung Fossil Site, a monument in North West.

name: West Coast Fossil Park
description: West Coast Fossil Park, a monument in Western Cape.

name: Klasies River Cave
description: Klasies River Cave, a monument in Eastern Cape.

name: Kaditshwene Cultural Landscape
description: Kaditshwene Cultural Landscape, a monument in North West.

name: Makapans Valley
description: Makapans Valley, a monument in Limpopo.

name: Bushmanskloof Rock Painting Landscape
description: Bushmanskloof Rock Painting Landscape, a monument in Western Cape.

name: SS Mendi Memorial
description: SS Mendi Memorial, a monument in Western Cape.

name: Fish Traps
description: Fish Traps, a monument in Eastern Cape.

name: South African Astronomical Observatory
description: South African Astronomical Observatory, a building in Western Cape.

name: Walter Sisulu Square
description: Walter Sisulu Square, a monument in Gauteng.

name: Sibhudu Cave
description: Sibhudu Cave, a monument in KwaZulu-Natal.

name: Owl House and Camel Yard
description: Owl House and Camel Yard, a building in Eastern Cape.

name: Sharpeville Massacre
description: Sharpeville Massacre, a monument in Gauteng.

name: Freedom Square
description: Freedom Square, a monument in Gauteng.

name: Stone Wall
description: Stone Wall, a monument in Eastern Cape.

name: Chief Tyali Grave
description: Chief Tyali Grave, a monument in Eastern Cape.

name: Lake Fundudzi
description: Lake Fundudzi, a monument in Limpopo.

name: Queen Victoria Statue (Pietermaritzburg)
description: Bronze statue of Queen Victoria on a marble plinth, located outside the Pietermaritzburg City Hall. Erected in 1901 to commemorate the Boer War. Sculpted by Herbert Hampton.

name: Queen Victoria Statue (Port Elizabeth)
description: Bronze statue of Queen Victoria in front of the Port Elizabeth City Hall. One of several statues of Queen Victoria erected in South Africa after her death. Sculpted by Edward Onslow Ford.

name: Louis Botha Statue (Cape Town)
description: Equestrian bronze statue of General Louis Botha, first Prime Minister of the Union of South Africa, located on the Foreshore near the Cape Town City Hall.

name: Jan Smuts Statue (Cape Town)
description: Bronze statue of Jan Smuts, South African statesman and Field Marshal, located on the Cape Town Foreshore opposite the entrance to the City Hall.

name: Cecil Rhodes Statue (Cape Town)
description: Bronze statue of Cecil John Rhodes on horseback, located at the bottom of Devil's Peak in the Company's Garden. The statue looks north towards the interior of Africa.

name: Mahatma Gandhi Statue (Johannesburg)
description: Bronze statue of Mahatma Gandhi in Gandhi Square, Johannesburg. Commemorates Gandhi's time in South Africa (1893-1914) where he developed his philosophy of satyagraha.

name: Steve Biko Statue (Johannesburg)
description: Bronze statue of anti-apartheid activist Steve Biko located outside the Newtown Cultural Precinct in Johannesburg. Shows Biko seated, lost in thought.

name: Shaka Zulu Statue (Durban)
description: Large bronze statue of King Shaka Zulu standing tall with a spear and shield, located outside the Durban International Convention Centre. Commemorates the great Zulu king.

name: Chief Dingaan Statue (Durban)
description: Bronze statue of King Dingane kaSenzangakhona, Zulu king, located in Durban. Represents the controversial Zulu monarch who fought against the Voortrekkers.

name: King Sekhukhune Statue (Pretoria)
description: Bronze statue of King Sekhukhune I of the BaPedi, located near Church Square in Pretoria. Commemorates the Pedi king who resisted Boer and British colonialism.

name: Albert Luthuli Statue (Johannesburg)
description: Bronze statue of Albert Luthuli, ANC president and Nobel Peace Prize laureate, located in Luthuli House, Johannesburg. Symbolizes his peaceful resistance to apartheid.

name: Oliver Tambo Statue (Johannesburg)
description: Bronze statue of Oliver Tambo, ANC leader and anti-apartheid activist, located outside the ANC headquarters in Johannesburg. Shows Tambo in a reflective pose.

name: Joe Slovo Statue (Johannesburg)
description: Bronze statue of Joe Slovo, anti-apartheid leader and SACP leader, located in Johannesburg. One of the few statues of a white communist in South Africa.

name: Ruth First Statue (Johannesburg)
description: Bronze statue of Ruth First, anti-apartheid activist and scholar, located at the University of the Witwatersrand. Commemorates her life and assassination.

name: Solomon Mahlangu Statue (Pretoria)
description: Bronze statue of Solomon Mahlangu, Umkhonto we Sizwe operative executed in 1979, located in Mahlangu Square, Pretoria. Symbolizes the youth sacrifice against apartheid.

name: Statue of Cecil John Rhodes
description: Bronze statue of Cecil John Rhodes seated on a horse, located in Company's Garden, Cape Town.
source: Wikipedia / Statue of Cecil John Rhodes

name: Statue of Jan van Riebeeck
description: Bronze statue of Jan van Riebeeck, the Dutch colonial administrator who founded Cape Town, located on Heerengracht, Cape Town.
source: Wikipedia / Statue of Jan van Riebeeck

name: Nelson Mandela Statue (Johannesburg)
description: 9-meter bronze statue of Nelson Mandela standing on the balcony of Johannesburg City Hall, where he gave his first public speech after release from prison.

name: Mahatma Gandhi Statue
description: Statue of Mahatma Gandhi located in Gandhi Square, Johannesburg, commemorating his time in South Africa.

name: Shaka Zulu Statue
description: Bronze statue of King Shaka Zulu on the Durban beachfront, overlooking the Indian Ocean.

name: Steve Biko Statue
description: Statue of anti-apartheid activist Steve Biko located in Grahamstown (Makhanda), Eastern Cape.

name: Statue of J.B.M. Hertzog
description: Statue of former Prime Minister James Barry Munnik Hertzog in Bloemfontein, Free State.

name: Statue of C.F. Swart
description: Statue of C.F. Swart, first State President of the Republic of South Africa, located in Bloemfontein.

name: Statue of Louis Botha
description: Equestrian statue of General Louis Botha, first Prime Minister of the Union of South Africa, located in Pretoria.

name: Jan Smuts Statue
description: Statue of Jan Smuts, South African statesman and military leader, located near the Union Buildings in Pretoria.

name: Statue of Andries Pretorius
description: Statue of Andries Pretorius, leader of the Voortrekkers, located in Church Square, Pretoria.

name: Statue of Sir George Grey
description: Statue of Sir George Grey, Governor of the Cape Colony, located in Cape Town.

name: Paul Kruger Statue
description: Bronze statue of Paul Kruger, President of the South African Republic, located at Kruger Gate near Hazyview.

name: Queen Victoria Statue
description: Statue of Queen Victoria located in Pietermaritzburg, KwaZulu-Natal.

name: Queen Victoria Statue (Port Elizabeth)
description: Statue of Queen Victoria located in Port Elizabeth, Eastern Cape.
